rulings.Case Title: JEANETTE TACKETT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cahill Construction Co., Inc. To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents, And Plaintiffs¿ Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Cahill Construction Co., Inc. to produce person most knowledgeable and custodian of records for deposition and responsive documents is denied. Plaintiffs¿ motion is properly set for hearing on or before the 15th day before the trial date as required by C.C.P. 2024.020. However, pursuant to C.C.P. 2024.020, discovery is closed and the court cannot compel defendant to conduct discovery proceedings after the close of discovery when plaintiffs did not move to complete discovery proceedings closer to the trial date or re-open discovery. (See Pelton-Shepherd Industries v. Delta Packaging Products (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568.) Even if the court could compel discovery to be completed, plaintiffs¿ motion would be denied. Defendant is a dissolved corporation and is under no obligation to produce a deponent. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Scott Company Of California To Verify Its Responses And For Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production, Requests For Admission; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel Scott Co. of California, on whose behalf Intervenors have intervened, to provide verifications to its responses to plaintiff¿s special interrogatories, requests for production and requests for admission is off calendar as moot. Verifications have been provided. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories nos. 36-98, requests for production nos. 1-61 and requests for admission nos. 1-82 is off calendar as moot as such responses have been served. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. Intervenors¿ responsive brief was timely. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE MORRIS et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Scott Company Of California
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel the depositions of the persons most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Scott Co. of California and production of documents is granted. The relevant time period remains 1970-1990. The scope of the meaning of ¿GEOGRAPHIC AREA¿ will be limited as follows: Intervenors are only required to produce information and documents for those specific named sites and buildings identified on Exhibit A to plaintiff¿s deposition notice (i.e. U.C. Berkeley Biochemistry Building, Berkeley, California and Gyspum Building, San Leandro, CA). Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY B. BARNES et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Dana Companies LLC¿s motion to compel plaintiffs to provide further, verified responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories, sets one and two, form interrogatories, set one, and to produce responsive documents pursuant to defendant¿s request for product of documents, set one, and request for sanctions of $2965 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
